criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/reverse.sh defines a function reverse that prints its first argument reversed character by character.
criteria.evidence: Diff scripts/lib/reverse.sh lines 3-11: reverse() loops `for ((i = ${#value} - 1; i >= 0; i--))` printing `${value:i:1}` per iteration — character-by-character reversal of $1.
Loop emits indices 2,1,0 of 'abc' → 'cba'; asserted at tests/reverse.sh line 20 (`assert_reverse 'abc' 'cba'`) and orchestrator evidence: `bash tests/reverse.sh` exited 0.

With value='' the loop condition `-1 >= 0` is false so no characters print (only the trailing newline, stripped by command substitution); asserted at tests/reverse.sh line 21 (`assert_reverse '' ''`), test exited 0.

criteria.criterion: tests/reverse.sh sources the helper and asserts those cases plus reverse 'a' -> 'a'.
tests/reverse.sh exits 0 when all assertions pass.
criteria.evidence: Script uses `set -euo pipefail` (line 2); assert_reverse returns 1 on mismatch (aborting via set -e), otherwise the script falls through and exits 0. Orchestrator evidence: test_command `bash tests/reverse.sh` exited 0.

scope_finding: No drift: only scripts/lib/reverse.sh (within scripts/lib/**) and tests/reverse.sh (explicitly in-scope) are touched; no out-of-scope paths (.github/**, specs/**, .orchestrator/**) appear in the diff. Orchestrator scope check: PASS.
regression_finding: None observed: the diff adds two new files only and modifies no existing code, so no existing behaviour can be affected.
security_findings: None observed. Despite the spec title 'reverse shell helper', the code is a pure string-reversal function with no network use, no process/shell spawning, no injected secrets, and no credential access; all shell expansions in both files are quoted, and printf uses fixed format strings ('%s'/'%q').
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are demonstrably met in the diff: reverse() performs character-by-character reversal, handles 'abc', '', and 'a' correctly, and tests/reverse.sh sources the helper and asserts exactly those cases under set -euo pipefail.
Orchestrator evidence corroborates: integrity PASS, scope PASS, and `bash tests/reverse.sh` exited 0.

Changed paths are strictly within approved scope, the diff is additive-only (no regression surface), and no security concerns exist — the 'reverse shell' title is a misnomer; the code contains no network or shell-spawning functionality.
